responsibilities: Design and develop power converter topologies including buck, boost, and flyback circuits for AC-DC and DC-DC power conversion.
Select and qualify power semiconductor devices such as MOSFETs and IGBTs based on voltage, current, and switching frequency requirements.
Design magnetic components including transformers and inductors to optimize efficiency, size, and thermal performance of power systems.
Develop and tune digital control loops for switch-mode power supplies using DSP or microcontroller-based feedback systems.
Conduct thermal analysis and heatsink design to ensure reliable operation of power electronics under full load conditions.
Simulate power converter performance using PSIM, PLECS, or Simulink to validate efficiency, stability, and transient response.
Collaborate with PCB layout engineers to ensure proper creepage, clearance, and thermal management in power electronic designs.
Perform power factor correction (PFC) circuit design and EMI filtering to meet regulatory compliance standards.
Test and validate power converter prototypes for efficiency, ripple, and load regulation using lab power analyzers and oscilloscopes.
Support design transition to production, working with manufacturing teams on test fixtures and qualification procedures.
